agents.name: commit
agents.label: Commit
agents.description: Generate and apply a conventional commit

agents.name: standup
agents.label: Standup
agents.description: Generate daily standup from git activity

agents.name: review
agents.label: Code Review
agents.description: Two-axis review (Standards + Spec) of changes since a fixed point

agents.name: test
agents.label: Test Writer
agents.description: Write tests for a file or function

agents.name: scout
agents.label: Repo Scout
agents.description: Map repo structure, find symbols, and trace code flow
guidelines: Use scout automatically when you need to inspect unfamiliar code, map relevant files, or gather repo context before editing.
Use scout instead of spending expensive main-model turns on broad file discovery.
Do not use scout for trivial single-file reads or when the relevant files are already known.

agents.name: diff_review
agents.label: Diff Review
agents.description: Review changed code for correctness bugs and regressions
guidelines: Use diff_review automatically after non-trivial edits or when reviewing staged/branch changes.
Use diff_review for correctness, regression, edge-case, and maintainability review; avoid style-only comments.
Do not use diff_review for tiny one-line edits unless high risk.

agents.name: tests
agents.label: Test Strategist
agents.description: Find missing tests and high-risk test cases
guidelines: Use tests automatically after adding or changing behavior to identify missing high-value tests.
Use tests before writing tests when framework, fixtures, or existing test style are unknown.
Do not use tests for documentation-only changes.

agents.name: security
agents.label: Security Review
agents.description: Check code for exploitable security issues
guidelines: Use security automatically for auth, permissions, input parsing, file/network/process execution, secrets, payments, or data exposure changes.
Use security to trace sources to sinks and report exploitable issues only.
Do not use security for purely visual or copy changes.
